PROP-026_open_issues_backlog_clearance.verification_pattern_check_GAP: Matrix has only 3 actions (PATCH/WONTFIX/ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN); 100% over-escalation on first fire — this PROP-027 amends to add 2 routing actions

findings.description: PROP-026 M1 matrix has only three actions (PATCH | WONTFIX | ESCALATE-TO-HUMAN). First burndown fire at 2026-05-10T07:58Z processed 17 candidates and over-escalated 9 of 9 moderate-severity items to pending-human, all of which were analyst-class work (not operator-only). Operator manually re-routed the 9 to assigned-analyst at 08:25Z (commit d44e2670).
findings.diagnosis: Definitional gap, not contingent failure. The matrix's 'moderate severity → escalate' default forces over-escalation when the moderate is analyst-class. The fix is structural — add ROUTE-TO-ANALYST as a first-class action with ROUTE-TO-ANALYST as the new default for moderates.

findings.description: When M1 routes to analyst and analyst eventually authors an EXP that re-enters the curmudgeon queue, the EXP's review_class is the load-bearing tag for PROP-025 batchability. Without an upstream class_hint signal, analyst defaults to deciding review_class from EXP scope alone — losing PROP-025 throughput when the routing context already implied 'verification' or 'deep-attack'.
findings.evidence: Operator's directive Q7 explicitly flagged this. PROP-025 makes review_class authoritative on the EXP file, but provides no upstream advisory signal; PROP-027 adds class_hint as that signal.
findings.diagnosis: PROP-025 covers the EXP→queue→curmudgeon half of the chain. PROP-027 fills the M1→ISS→analyst half.

findings.description: Closure-ledger schema needs new action_taken values ('route-to-analyst', 'route-to-curmudgeon', 'narrow-patch') for audit trail consistency and revert-script support.
findings.evidence: Current ledger entries use closure_evidence.action_intent free-text; no top-level structured action_taken field. Revert script can't distinguish closure types.
